programme.title: NFL Game Re-Airs
programme.desc: Despite a 15 point fourth quarter from the Indianapolis Colts, the Pittsburgh Steelers ended with 21-18 victory to advance to the AFC title game.

programme.title: NFL Throwback
programme.desc: Brett Kollmann breaks down how the Butt Fumble was caused by events that took place 36 years prior.

programme.title: NFL Throwback
programme.desc: See which NFL superstars were shockingly traded at the peak of their careers.

programme.title: NFL Game Re-Airs
programme.desc: During the matchup between the New Orleans Saints and the Minnesota Vikings, a last-minute 61-yard touchdown by Stefon Diggs gave Minnesota a 29-24 victory over New Orleans to send the Vikings to the NFC championship game.

programme.title: NFL Game Re-Airs
programme.desc: Relive the 2018 AFC Championship matchup between the New England Patriots and the Kansas City Chiefs where Brady blew through Kansas City's exhausted defense on a 75-yard drive to Rex Burkhead's 2-yard touchdown run in a 37-31 victory Sunday for the AFC championship.

programme.title: NFL Game Re-Airs
programme.desc: Aaron Rodgers throws a 36-yard pass to a toe-dragging Jared Cook on the sideline, and Mason Crosby kicked a 51-yard field goal as time expired to defeat the Cowboys, sending the Packers to the NFC Championship game.

programme.title: NFL Game Re-Airs
programme.desc: Relive the 2009 NFC Wild Card Round matchup between the Green Bay Packers and the Arizona Cardinals where Kurt Warner and playoff newcomer Aaron Rodgers staged the highest-scoring postseason game in league history ended abruptly in overtime. Karlos Dansby returned Rodgers' fumble 17 yards for a touchdown to give the Cardinals a 51-45 victory over the Packers.

programme.title: NFL Media Originals
programme.desc: This Pride Month, the NFL honors Richard Fierro for his acts of bravery as he and other survivors of the share their stories of healing, the importance of safe spaces and their hope for unity in the futures.

programme.title: NFL 360
programme.desc: Raiders linebacker Carl Nassib comes out as gay and helps transform lives, and Super Bowl champion Osi Umenyiora helps Nigerian prospects escape dramatic hardships.

programme.title: NFL Films Presents
programme.desc: Jeff Rohrer, Esera Tuaolo, and Vince Lombardi are LGBTQ+ allys in sports, advocating for visibility and acceptance. Rohrer made history as the first openly gay NFL player, while Tuaolo courageously came out after retiring. Lombardi's legacy extends beyond football, fostering a culture of inclusivity. Celebrate their contributions during Pride Month.

programme.title: NFL Game Re-Airs
programme.desc: Watch the matchup between the New York Jets and the New England Patriots during the 2010 Divisional Round where Mark Sanchez threw three touchdown passes, Tom Brady was sacked five times. The New York Jets reached their second straight AFC championship game with a stunning 28-21 win over the New England Patriots.

programme.title: A Football Life
programme.desc: Identical twins Tiki and Ronde Barber dominate the game of football but end their careers in very different ways.

programme.title: NFL Game Re-Airs
programme.desc: Tiki Barber ran for 234 yards and three touchdowns as he carried the New York Giants offense to a 34-28 victory over the Washington Redskins.

programme.title: NFL Mic'd Up
programme.desc: Super Bowl Champion Peyton Manning as heard on the field and sidelines.

programme.title: Super Bowl Classics
programme.desc: Von Miller and the Denver defense torment Panthers quarterback Cam Newton all game long and the Broncos make enough big plays for a 24-10 victory, in what would be the last one of Peyton Manning's career. From Feb. 7, 2016.

programme.title: A Football Life
programme.desc: From Detroit to Notre Dame, Los Angeles to Pittsburgh and finally to Canton, a grand tour on the only "Bus" to stop at the Pro Football Hall of Fame.

programme.title: NFL Game Re-Airs
programme.desc: Jerome Bettis rushed for 101 yards and two touchdowns and Ben Roethlisberger was efficient against the NFL's top-ranked defense as the Pittsburgh Steelers posted a critical 21-9 victory over the Chicago Bears.

programme.title: A Football Life
programme.desc: Hall of Fame WR Calvin Johnson reveals the truth behind his 40-yard dash at the 2007 NFL Combine and breaking Jerry Rice's record.

programme.title: NFL Game Re-Airs
programme.desc: Matthew Stafford threw one of four touchdown passes to Calvin Johnson and found the star receiver for a 57-yard play that set up Matt Prater's 27-yard field goal with 2:29 left in overtime, helping the Lions beat the Bears 37-34.

programme.title: A Football Life
programme.desc: A camera crew follows Ray Lewis for an entire season on and off the field.

programme.title: NFL Game Re-Airs
programme.desc: Relive the final game of Hall of Fame linebacker Ray Lewis' legendary career, as he and quarterback Joe Flacco guided the Baltimore Ravens to a thrilling win over the San Francisco 49ers in Super Bowl XLVII.

programme.title: Through X
programme.desc: Paul Carcaterra heads to Evanston, IL to visit with Northwestern's Izzy Scane. Izzy has been a dominant force for the Wildcats since she first arrived on campus. With a National title, the Tewaaraton Award winner, and record shattering season – she has cemented herself as an all-time great. We also went to Charlottesville, VA to dissect the elite game of Connor Shellenberger: a hometown hero, and arguably the best player in history for the University of Virginia.

programme.title: Mitchell Pehlke Lacrosse Show
programme.desc: Welcome to Episode 16 (Season 3) of the Mitchell Pehlke Lacrosse Show presented by Duke Cannon. In this episode, Mitch and the boys discuss everything that happened in the PLL during Charlotte Weekend, including CJ Kirst's 6-goal performance in a Philadelphia Waterdogs revenge win over the Boston Cannons. They also react to some proposed rule changes in College Lacrosse, then James Hogan provides an update on the NCAA Transfer Portal. Utah Archers goalie Brett Dobson joins the show for an interview.

programme.title: Marcus Holman and the Moment He Became Obsessed with Lacrosse
programme.desc: Kyle Harrison sits down with Marcus Holman - one of lacrosse’s greatest shooters and leaders - for an honest conversation about family, obsession, adversity, longevity, and chasing the all-time goals record. Marcus takes us back to his earliest memories from John's Hopkins’ locker room to the moment he fell in love with shooting He opens up about evolving his role, becoming a leader, sustaining greatness into his mid-30s, and what it would mean to cement his legacy in the record books.

programme.title: University of Texas Documentary
programme.desc: 3 years ago, pro lacrosse player Kyle Hartzell stepped on the campus of University of Texas at Austin as their new club lacrosse team head coach. Now they are a top MCLA program competing for championships. TLN went to Austin, TX during the most anticipated game of the Longhorn's season to uncover what makes their program so special.

programme.title: Going West
programme.desc: University of Utah turns club lacrosse into NCAA lacrosse team

programme.title: TLN Facility Tours
programme.desc: Johns Hopkins & Clemson: Explore two top-tier lacrosse programs as TLN takes you inside their world-class facilities. Visit Johns Hopkins in Baltimore, MD, home to the legendary Blue Jays, and tour the iconic Cordish Lacrosse Center and Homewood Field. Then, head to Clemson, SC, for an exclusive look at the nation's premier women's lacrosse facilities.

programme.title: Through X
programme.desc: Joey Spallina - Paul Carcaterra visits Syracuse, N.Y. to pull back the curtain on the biggest name in College Lacrosse: Joey Spallina. In this episode of THROUGH X, Spallina speaks on wearing the No. 22 at Syracuse, shares his goal to return the Orange to NCAA Championship glory, and shows you how to score his signature behind-the-back goal.

programme.desc: Legendary lawman Clay Griffin is losing his eyesight and refuses an operation despite Annie's pleading.

programme.desc: Eastern doctor's attempts to prevent a smallpox epidemic gets hampered

programme.desc: A fight for tolerance and brotherhood in San Francisco by Lord Charlie.

programme.desc: Patrick and McGivern look for a gold shipment stolen by a poetic bandit.

programme.title: The Californians
programme.desc: The cowardly justice of the peace must face outlaw Blackie Stuart.

programme.title: The Quick and the Dead
programme.desc: In 1876 Wyoming, a mysterious stranger enters the lives of a homesteading couple after they are attacked by a ruthless gang.

programme.title: The Bounty Hunter
programme.desc: Jim Kipp (Randolph Scott) is a bounty hunter hired to find three robbers who committed a daring heist three years ago. When he arrives in a small town near the robbery, he senses that the thieves may be living there. However, many of the townspeople -- including Dr. Spencer (Harry Antrim), his daughter, Julie (Dolores Dorn), and barmaid Alice (Marie Windsor) -- are put on edge by Kipp's presence and want him gone. Kipp perseveres, hatching a plan that he thinks will draw out the bandits.

programme.title: Two Mules for Sister Sara
programme.desc: When a drifter saves a nun on the run, they join forces with revolutionaries resisting the French occupation of Mexico and become good friends.

programme.title: Joe Kidd
programme.desc: A gunslinger is hired by a wealthy land baron to stop the rebellion of local Mexican revolutionaries who claim they’ve been cheated out of their land.

programme.title: High Plains Drifter
programme.desc: A gunslinging “Man with No Name” rides into the lawless Western settlement of Lago and makes a name for himself with a string of blazing battles.

programme.title: The War Wagon
programme.desc: An ex-convict and his partner eye an armored stagecoach full of gold fitted with a Gatling gun for protection.

programme.title: Red Sun
programme.desc: During an 1870 train heist, a gang steals a ceremonial Japanese sword meant as a gift for the U.S. President and prompts a manhunt for its recovery.

programme.title: The Gene Autry Show
programme.desc: Ten-year-old violin prodigy Reginald Redaldo runs away to the Flying A Ranch, unaware that a dishonest ranch hand will steal his Stradivarius.

programme.title: The Gene Autry Show
programme.desc: Gene provides protection for the stagecoach to San Dimas, which rolls through outlaw and hostile Indian territory.

programme.title: The Gene Autry Show
programme.desc: A portrait painter plans crimes while sketching his models, who are chosen to help the artist gain financial reward.

programme.title: Thunder Over the Plains
programme.desc: It's the late 1860s, and the U.S. government has yet to reclaim Texas as a state. A group of opportunistic and amoral Northerners head south to claim the territory as their own. Though Texas lawman Capt. Porter (Randolph Scott) believes it's his duty to honor the local leaders, he can't ignore the corruption of the Yankee politicians who are giving him orders. When Confederate veteren Ben (Charles McGraw) is unjustly sentenced to death, Porter decides he has had enough of his new bosses.

programme.title: Riding Shotgun
programme.desc: Larry Delong (Randolph Scott) loses his sister and her son when they're gunned down by the notorious Dan Marady (James Millican) and his band of outlaws during a holdup. Delong takes a job as a stagecoach guard, hoping to run into the bandits and take revenge. Run into them he does, but numbers are on Marady's side, and they shoot up and rob Delong's coach. Delong barely escapes with his life. When he tries to alert the authorities, they implicate him in the crime, and he becomes a fugitive.

programme.title: The Man Behind the Gun
programme.desc: An Army major (Randolph Scott) goes undercover to stop a plot to make Southern California a separate state.

programme.title: Sierra
programme.desc: A tomboy frontier lawyer (Wanda Hendrix) finds an outlaw (Dean Jagger) and his son (Audie Murphy) hiding out from a false charge of murder.

programme.title: The Oklahoman
programme.desc: While moving his family out West, Dr. John Brighton (Joel McCrea) loses his wife in childbirth. After her death, John sets up his home in Indian territory. By the time his daughter is 7 years old, two women are competing for the doctor's affections: Ann (Barbara Hale), a rancher's widow, and Maria (Gloria Talbott), a young Native American maiden who watches over the doctor's daughter. When the town bully, Mel (Douglas Dick), wants to take the land of Maria's father, the doctor gets involved.

programme.title: Westbound
programme.desc: A former Union officer, John Hayes (Randolph Scott) now manages a stagecoach company. However, Clay Putnam (Andrew Duggan), a businessman still loyal to the South, is intent on robbing the coaches operated by Hayes, which transport gold to the North from California. Hoping to heist the treasure as a way to revive the Confederacy, Putnam also has a grudge against Hayes, since his wife, Norma (Virginia Mayo), was once involved with the handsome Union soldier.

programme.title: Black Patch
programme.desc: A one-eyed marshal (George Montgomery) puts a bank-robbing buddy in jail, but two greedy locals try to get him out.

programme.desc: In the New Mexico Territory, a Civil War vet raises a son, tends to his ranch, and wields his trusty rifle to help enforce the laws of the wild west.

programme.title: Jinkx Monsoon: Red Head Redemption
programme.desc: Jinkx Monsoon recounts some of the crazier tales from her sex-addicted, alcoholic past, which led to her finding her true self.

programme.title: Believe: The Eddie Izzard Story
programme.desc: Get to know the trailblazing comedian’s story: from losing her mother at an early age and being raised in a boarding school, to where she’s at now.

programme.title: Cameron Esposito: Marriage Material
programme.desc: A stand-up special by comedian Cameron Esposito that sends up topics ranging from the secrets of women’s locker rooms to gun control.

programme.title: Cara Connors: Straight for Pay
programme.desc: Connors delivers an exuberant hour of full-body laughs and plenty of thought-provoking material in her breakout debut comedy special.

programme.title: Daniel Webb: Hoe's Parade Live at the Rose Bowl
programme.desc: The comic discusses his frustrations with ​​Karen Pence, his loving relationship with his dog, and how jazzed he is to perform at the historic venue.

programme.title: Ilana Glazer: The Planet Is Burning
programme.desc: “Broad City” star Ilana Glazer steps up in her first standup comedy set, done in her own raucous style as she takes on Nazis, diva cups, and stoners.

programme.title: Jay Mohr: Altamont
programme.desc: Comedian Jay Mohr tackles subjects from unexpected visitors, bringing up children to working with Chris Farley on SNL in his new stand-up special.

programme.title: Jay Mohr: American Treasure
programme.desc: The popular comedian tells deeply personal stories from his fascinating life and presents his famously funny celebrity impressions and observations.

programme.title: Jill-Michele Meleán: White/Latina
programme.desc: The half-Bolivian, half-Irish comic talks about navigating vastly different cultural identities while creating her own brand of funny.

programme.title: Jim Gaffigan: The Pale Tourist - Canadian American
programme.desc: Jim Gaffigan performs in Canada and has some very strong opinions on poutine, Saskatchewan, health care and the pronunciation of the city of Regina.

programme.title: Jim Gaffigan: Quality Time
programme.desc: The comedian discusses why we aren’t honest about not wanting to attend events, lying to children, and so much more in this hilarious special.

programme.title: Kevin Hart Presents: Lil Rel Howery - RELevent
programme.desc: Hart hosts the high-energy comic who riffs on his upbringing, raising kids, and public transportation, proving he can be hilarious and relevant.

programme.title: Monét X Change: Fist of Glory
programme.desc: Join Queen Monét X Change in her debut stand-up special where she delivers dazzling stories from her life and a bawdy opera performance.

programme.title: Sinbad: Make Me Wanna Holla
programme.desc: A stand-up special covering the everything from the life advice his parents gave him to people with no talent having clothing lines and more.

programme.title: Eddie Izzard: Sexie
programme.desc: Witness the comedian’s hilarious 2003 world tour, full of imaginative observations that were performed in front of an Eastbourne audience.

programme.title: Samantha Ruddy: Baseball Mom
programme.desc: Comedian Samantha Ruddy touches on topics like her experience of moving to the South, the names of marijuana strains, and her Italian-American roots.

programme.title: Shayne Smith: Alligator Boys
programme.desc: This comic delivers wild stories about everything, from a failed robbery, to a wrestling match in the NYC subway, and even about saving a dog’s life.

programme.title: Todd Glass Talks About Stuff
programme.desc: In this caustic comedy special, veteran stand-up quipster Todd Glass makes audiences howl with his take on some of society's unspoken "rules," from complimenting kids to giving directions.

programme.title: Colin Quinn: Unconstitutional
programme.desc: Comedian Colin Quinn analyzes the constitutional history of the United States.

programme.title: Brian Posehn: The Fartist
programme.desc: Quirky comedian and actor Brian Posehn shares his stories of trying to be a better person in this stand-up set filmed live at Seattle's Neptune Theater.

programme.title: Leanne Morgan: So Yummy
programme.desc: The stand up comic discusses dogs, cheerleading camp, the struggles of being an empty nester, and joining Weight Watchers for the 7th time.

programme.title: Alonzo Bodden: Heavy Lightweight
programme.desc: Last Comic Standing winner Alonzo Bodden straddles the line between deep and droll with hilarious commentary on Kanye, Alexa, and our absurd reality.

programme.title: Alonzo Bodden: Who's Paying Attention
programme.desc: “Last Comic Standing” winner Alonzo Bodden jokes about outlandish pop culture, as well as spoofing well-known stereotypes, in this live special.

programme.title: Alonzo Bodden: Tall, Dark and Funny
programme.desc: Alonzo hilariously touches upon every subject from tsunamis to terrorists, Democrats to desperate housewives, marriage to midgets, and more,

programme.title: Alonzo Bodden: Historically Incorrect
programme.desc: The New York native and Last Comic Standing winner examines historical inaccuracies of the past, which have led to our present situation.

programme.title: Chad Daniels: As Is
programme.desc: Recorded live at the Acme Comedy Co. in Minneapolis Chad Daniels tackles such topics as airline travel, his kids and puberty. Uncut & uncensored!

programme.title: Ian Harvie: May the Best Cock Win
programme.desc: Ian Harvie is not quite the kind of man you might think. He's redefining what it means to be a dude through a fresh perspective on sex/sexuality with some seriously funny and new kinds of dick jokes. Unafraid to quip about subjects no other comedian has ever touched, Ian’s performance challenges the traditionally macho, sex-obsessed world of stand up in ways you won’t believe, proving that laughter cuts across all identities and ultimately unites us all in MAY THE BEST COCK WIN. Oh and, Ian is the world's first trans man comedian with a stand up comedy special.

programme.title: Jimmy O. Yang: Good Deal
programme.desc: The Silicon Valley star uses his debut stand-up special to discuss tall women, disappointing his parents, and how he learned English from rap videos.

programme.title: Keep Your Distance: Vol. 17
programme.desc: KevOnStage hosts a night of stand-up comedy featuring Tony Valentino and Brennon Edwards.

programme.title: Louie Anderson: Big Underwear
programme.desc: The iconic comic plays on the challenges of getting healthy, life on the road, dealing with technology and how we all eventually become our parents.

programme.title: Louie Anderson: Big Baby Boomer
programme.desc: Veteran funny man Louie Anderson returns to the stage in this hilarious one hour stand up comedy special. Louie takes an unconventional and jovial look at his bad habits, pesky family members, and aging body.

programme.title: Tom Segura: Completely Normal
programme.desc: Radio, television and live performance veteran Tom Segura shares a few off-hand appraisals on hotels and hobbies with a cool and thoughtful demeanor.

programme.title: Jeff Dunham: Spark of Insanity
programme.desc: Jeff and his felt friends Walter, Melvin the Superhero Guy, Peanut, and others share their latest and greatest thoughts about our times.

programme.title: Jeff Dunham: Unhinged in Hollywood
programme.desc: Previously unseen stand-up comedy by Jeff Dunham; sketches that include popular characters; Brad Paisley performs; special appearances by comic Chris Parnell and former UFC champion Chuck Liddell.

programme.title: Jim Gaffigan: Noble Ape
programme.desc: The stand-up comedy star describes how his wife faced a medical crisis, and he attempted to hold down the fort for their five children.

programme.title: The Johnny Carson Show
programme.desc: Christie Brinkley on photo shoots all over the world, and skydiving, but chickening out at the last minute.

programme.desc: "The Hellcats" (1969) Crime drama. A slain policeman's brother and girlfriend infiltrate a motorcycle gang of drug smugglers. With Ross Hagen, Dee Duffy, Sharyn Kinzie.

programme.desc: An intergalactic cop pursues a corrupt former partner; comedic commentary by Mike, Kevin and Bill.

programme.desc: "The Sidehackers" a k a "Five the Hard Way" (1969) Adventure. A motorcycle racer rejects the wrong woman and nearly pays with his life.

programme.title: Mystery Science Theater 3000
programme.desc: A brave young knight and his comrades seek a lost weapon from Atlantis to help rid their land of an evil ruler in "Quest of the Delta Knights.".

programme.title: Mystery Science Theater 3000
programme.desc: A hit-and-run victim is brought back from the dead by a voodoo doctor, and then seeks revenge.

programme.desc: Kinga, drops a bombshell on Jonah before plunging him into a world of bare-chested carnies.

programme.desc: A motley group aboard a German U-boat discovers an island full of ancient beasts.

programme.desc: The crew watches "The Magic Voyage of Sinbad," where a sea-going adventurer sets sail to find the bluebird of happiness, which he believes will help his down-on-its-luck hometown.

programme.desc: "Eegah!" (1963) Horror. A prehistoric man abducts a teenage singer's girlfriend.

programme.title: Mystery Science Theater 3000
programme.desc: "Hobgoblins" (1985) Horror. Lethal little creatures escape from a studio vault.

programme.title: Mystery Science Theater 3000
programme.desc: "The Corpse Vanishes" (1942) Horror. A mad botanist drains kidnapped brides to keep his 80-year-old wife looking young. With Bela Lugosi, Luana Walters, Elizabeth Russell.

programme.title: Divided
programme.desc: Four strangers including a bartender and a personal trainer debate.

programme.title: Baggage
programme.desc: Corey may have sent a sex tape of his ex to her mother, may have been sent to jail for harboring a fugitive, or may have sent nude photos of himself to Playgirl and gotten rejected.

programme.title: Baggage
programme.desc: Jason may have been booted from a boy band, may be a biter in the bedroom, or may expect his girlfriend to bathe him each night.

programme.title: Baggage
programme.desc: Cibi may have shared his ex with five of his buddies, may have slept with five of his exes' mothers, or may disown his five children just to pick up women.

programme.title: Baggage
programme.desc: Elliot may have stolen money from his church, meat from the grocery store, or drugs from medical school.

programme.title: Mind of a Man
programme.desc: Women have been trying to answer the question of "what do men think" for years. "Mind of a Man" tries to help them figure out what is on the mind of the average guy. Each half-hour episode pits two female contestants against each other as they try to correctly answer questions asked of 100 men. Helping the women along the way is a rotating celebrity panel composed of a mix of men and women who offer opinions. The contestant who is more successful in understanding the mind of a man wins the game and advances to the bonus round, in which she can amass up to $10,000.

programme.title: Divided
programme.desc: Four strangers debate the answers to trivia and social survey questions, including what ranks as the biggest distractions among teenage drivers.

programme.title: Divided
programme.desc: Strangers debate against a racing clock about which traditional wedding vows that are the hardest for woman to keep.

programme.title: 25 Words or Less
programme.desc: Two teams comprised of celebrities and civilians against each other in a fast-paced word game with a top prize of $10,000

programme.title: 25 Words or Less
programme.desc: Two teams comprised of celebrities and civilians against each other in a fast-paced word game with a top prize of $10,000

programme.title: 1 vs. 100
programme.desc: Professional dancer Carrie Ann Inaba hosts this show in which a contestant can win $50,000. The contestant, known as the One, competes with a mob of 100 other players by answering questions. When the One answers correctly, each member of the mob that answered incorrectly is eliminated. The One earns money for each mob member who is eliminated. If the One can eliminate the entire mob, he wins $50,000. But if the One gets a question wrong, he is eliminated and the remaining mob members split his winnings.

programme.title: Tug of Words
programme.desc: Based on the playground game Tug-O-War, teams of two compete to answer word-clue questions correctly and pull the flag on the tug of war rope to their side.

programme.title: Tug of Words

programme.title: Family Feud
programme.desc: Steve Harvey assumes the hosting duties for this daytime game show in which five members of one family are pitted against five members of another family. Each team's goal is to guess the results of audience survey questions. A player from each team initially faces off, answering the question to earn the chance to either pass or play. The winning family gets a chance to earn extra cash in a bonus round.

programme.title: 1 vs. 100
programme.desc: Professional dancer Carrie Ann Inaba hosts this show in which a contestant can win $50,000. The contestant, known as the One, competes with a mob of 100 other players by answering questions. When the One answers correctly, each member of the mob that answered incorrectly is eliminated. The One earns money for each mob member who is eliminated. If the One can eliminate the entire mob, he wins $50,000. But if the One gets a question wrong, he is eliminated and the remaining mob members split his winnings.

programme.title: Stepfather II
programme.desc: After cheating death by surviving being both stabbed and shot, psychotic killer Jerry (Terry O'Quinn) is placed in a mental institution, but he soon murders his doctor and escapes. Taking on a dead man's identity, he moves to an upscale neighborhood in Los Angeles and sets his sights on local woman Carol (Meg Foster), slowly winning her heart. Carol's son, Todd (Jonathan Brandis), grows suspicious of his mom's new lover, however, and tensions reach the boiling point at the couple's wedding.

programme.desc: An escaped madman (Robert Wightman) romances a California divorcee; luckily her son knows how to use a computer.

But what Boone doesn't know is that Decker is really a serial killer. Decker frames Boone to take the fall for his murders, and Boone is killed by the police. But Boone is brought back to life by the monsters of his dreams, the Nightbreed, who in turn join Boone in his quest to stop Decker from killing again.

programme.title: Big Dreams, Small Spaces
programme.desc: In Brighton determined duo Helen and Dax have their hands full with twins, one of whom has special needs. Meanwhile in High Wycombe, newly single Valerie is looking for a fresh lease of life.

programme.title: Big Dreams, Small Spaces
programme.desc: Monty Don visits two sets of ambitious gardeners, each facing challenges on a very different scale. One is a tiny urban courtyard in Hackney and the other is a outdoor area for a community.

programme.title: Big Dreams, Small Spaces
programme.desc: Monty meets two sets of gardeners who have unimaginable ambitions for their outdoor spaces. In Essex Denise has never ventured beyond the decking area behind her house. Meanwhile in Ipswich, a father and daughter plan to build a terraced allotment.

programme.title: Big Dreams, Small Spaces
programme.desc: Monty comes to the rescue of two couples in need of help transforming their rubble filled back gardens into havens of peace and tranquillity. They are in serious need of help and advice but can Monty set them on the right track?

programme.title: Big Dreams, Small Spaces
programme.desc: Two gardeners, each living on their own, are hoping Monty can help them. In Blackpool, Alex dreams of turning her boring back yard into a magical enchanted forest. Also in Bristol, Ross has taken on a very over-grown allotment.

programme.title: Big Dreams, Small Spaces
programme.desc: In Leyton, Tariq and Sairah want to turn their dumping ground of a back yard into a sumptuous award-winning garden, suitable for entertaining friends and family. Both have competitive jobs in the banking sector and are used to winning at work but when it comes to gardening, they don't know where to begin.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Rachel de Thame joins Monty in Longmeadow and adds more medicinal planting to the herb garden, especially those that the bees love. Rachel also investigates how we can aid honey bee conservation and protect the nation's favourite pollinators.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don continues work in his courtyard, where he gives advice on plants which thrive on shady walls, sows root crops in the vegetable garden and catches up on work in his cottage garden.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty gets to work in the cutting garden, plants his tomatoes and brings pots of citrus out of the greenhouse and into the garden for the summer. Also, Carol Klein visits another of her gardening heroes, Penelope Hobhouse.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At the height of summer it's time to ensure that fruits and vegetables are given attention to ensure maximum cropping. Monty gives advice on the summer pruning of fruit trees as well as showing how to get the best from tomatoes and chillies. He also recommends plants which will carry on flowering into Autumn.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: There are plans for propagating and planting at Longmeadow when Monty plants up a new bed of irises and takes pelargonium cuttings. Nick brightens up a dull and shady space at the side of a terraced house and gives advice on plants that will thrive.

programme.title: Monty Don's Adriatic Gardens
programme.desc: Monty travels to Europe and explores the influence of the Venetians on gardening. To begin with, he explores Venice on one of the city's famous barges, stepping out to visit green spaces large and small that are often hidden from view behind high walls.

programme.title: Monty Don's Adriatic Gardens
programme.desc: Monty begins in the city of Trieste, close to Italy's border with Croatia, to visit a public garden first created by an obsessive royal plantsman more than 150 years ago, which is now being carefully restored. He then heads down the Adriatic coast through Croatia to pick up the trail that the Venetians followed, ending his journey on the beautiful island of Lopud, near Dubrovnik.

programme.title: Monty Don's Adriatic Gardens
programme.desc: Monty begins the last leg of his journey in Corfu, a Greek island with strong Venetian links, including olive trees planted by them that are still grown today. Here, Monty visits a spectacular garden made by an Englishman with Greek connections.

programme.title: My Dream Farm
programme.desc: Monty Don helps six different families realise their dream of returning to the land. They're all determined to leave the rat race behind and transform their lives. With over forty years' experience of farming, Monty Don is on hand to guide the novices through their first, tentative steps. The first episode features Dick and Pauline Beijen, who gave up their executive lifestyle and moved from Dick's native Holland to Devon.

programme.title: My Dream Farm
programme.desc: Having tires of their stressful city life, Rob and Sue Wood moved from the London suburbs to a windswept corner of Cornwall to pursue Rob's dream of living a more self-sufficient life. While renting a tiny smallholding costing them a whopping £1,200 a month, they hope to earn their living from the land. However, if that wasn't challenge enough, Sue is heavily pregnant with two young children already to care for.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don is at Longmeadow sharing a wealth of tips and ideas on how to keep your garden looking good all year long. He tackles some urgent pruning, and reveals some of the projects he's got in store over the coming months.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Hellebores, the jewels of the early spring border, come under the spotlight on this show. Monty Don shares his top tips on how to get the best from them. Carol Klein visits a couple who have just moved into a bungalow with a badly neglected garden.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don explains why he thinks that the once-popular chrysanthemum is worth revisiting and Joe Swift has some helpful suggestions if you're thinking of giving your garden a bit of a spring revamp.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don plants up a new bed of asparagus and shows us how to lift and divide hostas. On our second visit to South Africa, we explore the conditions that allow agapanthus to thrive in the wild.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don breaks ground for his new pond and Carol Klein pays Geoff and Sally Davis another visit in their Somerset garden. Their overgrown shrubs are in need of a jolly good haircut, but they haven't a clue where to begin.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As the weather begins to warm up, there's plenty to be getting on with in the garden. Monty Don welcomes us to Longmeadow and cracks on with some timely tasks. Including Water Iris Garden, Peonies, Seaside Garden, National Gardening Week.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Magnolias are the showstoppers of the tree world right now and to celebrate them in all their glory, James Alexander-Sinclair pays Borde Hill Garden in West Sussex, with its collection of magnificent champion trees, a visit.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Everything in the garden is shooting up so it's time to lift and divide perennials and Monty is doing just that with his ornamental grasses. In our third trip to South Africa, we discover where red hot pokers grow in the wild.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As Monty's wildlife garden begins to take shape at Longmeadow, bees are very much at the forefront of his mind. He plants up a nectar-rich border that will attract all sorts of bees and other pollinating insects.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: With the hanging basket season well and truly underway, Joe Swift meets a florist in Surrey who has perfected the art of kokedama, a modern take on the hanging basket. Houseplants come under the spotlight too.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: For the National Gardens Scheme, over 400 gardens in England and Wales will be opening to the public. Carol Klein visits one of them as they prepare for the big event. At Longmeadow a local beekeeper arrives with a swarm for Monty's new hive.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Carol Klein and Joe Swift are at Gardeners' World Live in Birmingham. Joe casts his expert eye over the spectacular show gardens, while Carol focuses on the delights of the floral marquee.

programme.desc: Monty begins the last leg of his journey in Corfu, a Greek island with strong Venetian links, including olive trees planted by them that are still grown today. Here, Monty visits a spectacular garden made by an Englishman with Greek connections.

programme.title: My Dream Farm
programme.desc: Monty Don helps six different families realise their dream of returning to the land. They're all determined to leave the rat race behind and transform their lives. With over forty years' experience of farming, Monty Don is on hand to guide the novices through their first, tentative steps. The first episode features Dick and Pauline Beijen, who gave up their executive lifestyle and moved from Dick's native Holland to Devon.

programme.title: My Dream Farm
programme.desc: Having tires of their stressful city life, Rob and Sue Wood moved from the London suburbs to a windswept corner of Cornwall to pursue Rob's dream of living a more self-sufficient life. While renting a tiny smallholding costing them a whopping £1,200 a month, they hope to earn their living from the land. However, if that wasn't challenge enough, Sue is heavily pregnant with two young children already to care for.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: Craig Harrison and Rusty Smith from Caza Outdoors (Los Cazadores, Pnuma,Trophy Scan, Heated Core & more) hunt with the TTHA crew at the G2 Ranch in Pearsall, TX. Sharing a deer camp with friends is a time honored tradition across the country, and one that we hold near and dear to our hearts here at Texas Trophy Hunters!

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: Throughout the years, our friendships have been forged in deer camps across the country, and this week is a continuation of some of those times. This week, we have Dave Peterson and Mike Winkler from Do All Outdoors and Jurassic Rock in camp with us. When all is said and done, we even got to do a little hunting ourselves and create more memories that will last a lifetime

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: We are blessed for the opportunity to follow along the guys from Trinity Oaks for Bucks for the Brave. The men and women that served in our armed services and as first responders, had jobs that required the, to be willing to sacrifice their own lives every day, in order to protect or save someone else's. Not many of us can say that, nor understand what that truly means. The things they've had to see, and the sacrifices they've made in their daily lives, can have a profound impact on them. Trinity Oaks' Bucks for the Brave platform allows the loved ones and communities that support these heroes to share their stories and unite us all in honoring and recognizing their service to our great nation. We are proud to continue to be a part of this incredible program, getting these heroes in the outdoors for the hunt of a lifetime.

programme.title: TRP
programme.desc: In this episode of the Tom Roland Podcast, brought to you by Danco, Captain Harley Hunt provides essential boating advice on how to determine if you're dragging anchor. Harley explains the importance of marking your GPS when setting the anchor and monitoring the distance to ensure you are stationary. He emphasizes the significance of re-anchoring if dragging occurs and shares the Coast Guard's recommended scope ratio for anchoring. Additionally, Harley discusses the benefits of using more chain to help the anchor dig in better. Tune in for valuable tips whether you're an experienced or beginner boater.Watch now to enhance your skills and improve your fishing game! programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: Mark Peterson sets his sights on a Free-Range New Zealand Red Stag, but it doesn't come easy. Dense fog, steep terrain, and thick vegetation make finding a mature stag no easy task. Watch as Mark Peterson and his guide cover the gorgeous New Zealand landscape in search of one of the most sought-after big game animals on the South Island of New Zealand. On this hunt, Mark was unable to get his firearms import permit in time to take his Gunwerks rifle, so he'd again have to borrow from his guide.

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: The team at Winchester Ammunition has arrived in Oregon to visit the Leupold Long Range Academy. There, they’ll put Winchester’s new “Supreme Long Range” ammunition to the test, pushing its performance across demanding long-range scenarios. With precision, consistency, and confidence on the line, the team is eager to demonstrate exactly what “Precision Rifle Perfected” means when every shot counts.

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: Join Benny Spies of Team Winchester as he shares stories of his upbringing in South Dakota. From cherished family photos to duck seasons of the past, learn how a passion for hunting was ignited and became a way of life for Benny and his family.

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: Over a decade after the game changing Longbeard XR was introduced by Winchester, the wait is finally over for their next ground breaking turkey load. After years of perfecting the recipe behind the scenes, Winchester finally unveiled the new Longbeard TSS at a turkey camp in northern Michigan. First watch the jaw dropping Longbeard TSS pattern board testing, then see it go to work on some spring gobblers.

programme.title: Behind the Draw
programme.desc: Mike makes a wild stalk on a beautiful velvet mule deer buck. The entire week was a week to remember filled with many ups and downs. We can't thank our buddy Cody enough for inviting us out! Don't miss Part 1 of this epic Montana bowhunt!

programme.title: Hunting Land Man
programme.desc: In this episode of Hunting Land Man, Asa is on a roll. She fills her second and third turkey tags of the year, capping off an incredible season and tagging out in Mississippi. The highlight of the week came when she got to experience her very first double-up with her dad, Slade. Sharing a moment like that in the turkey woods is something they'll never forget, making this a truly unforgettable hunt.

programme.title: Wildbound by Forloh
programme.desc: In this episode Andy goes back to his home farm in Alabama for deer and quail hunting. Not only does he make incredible dishes with his former ‘masterchef’ contestant Alex which he serves to local children in need but he explains how hunting is never about “just the hunt” but about the camaraderie, friendship and memories made through the pursuit of the harvest.

programme.title: Huntstand Presents
programme.desc: The West Texas desert has a way of pulling you in. In Desert Ghost, Will Cooper heads west to Marathon, Texas, with longtime friend and mentor Cody Roberts to hunt aoudad in one of the most remote landscapes in the state. Rooted in family legacy, this film explores Cody’s deep ties to the desert, the story behind Desert Ghost Distillery, and the mystique of a western oasis near Big Bend National Park. More than a hunt, Desert Ghost dives into the role of aoudad management and its importance to desert bighorn sheep conservation efforts led by Texas Parks and Wildlife and the Wild Sheep Foundation. Sometimes, to protect what belongs here — you have to chase the ghosts. You really do have to be tough, determined, or one lucky bastard to make it out in West Texas.

programme.title: The WoodHaven Way
programme.desc: Renowned turkey biologist Dr. Mike Chamberlain is hunting alongside Billy Yargus at Jay Scott Outdoors and connects on a beautiful, mature Gould’s gobbler.

programme.title: Pure Hunting
programme.desc: Willi and Chris have hunted NM for years with some success each year. This year they included two new friends, Nick Ventura and his dad. Willi had some success mid-hunt and with the clock winding down on the last evening, would the far off bugle produce a bull for Chris?

programme.title: Pure Hunting
programme.desc: Willi and Chris are back in New Mexico, bowhunting elk. Willi made a poor shot on a bull, and after discovering the cause of the poor shot, he hopes to get redemption as their hunting days come to a close.

programme.title: Hard Truths of Conservation
programme.desc: Famous for its Mayan ruins, the Yucatan in Mexico is home to a diverse array of wildlife. After years of over-harvesting and exploitation, regulated hunting is helping to conserve large areas of this fragile ecosystem. Dan Cabela travels to the region to find out more.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: It’s that time again—when the boys from StruttinBuck load up and head out of Arkansas, bound for Fairfield, Texas and the FE Hill Ranch. Another year, another hunt, and another chapter in a tradition that’s grown into something far bigger than the hunt itself. This annual father-son trip is built on early mornings, shared stories, and moments that stick with you long after the season ends. It’s the memories made over the years that keep calling them back—and this year will be no different.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: We are blessed for the opportunity to follow along the guys from Trinity Oaks for Bucks for the Brave. The men and women that served in our armed services and as first responders, had jobs that required the, to be willing to sacrifice their own lives every day, in order to protect or save someone else's. Not many of us can say that, nor understand what that truly means. The things they've had to see, and the sacrifices they've made in their daily lives, can have a profound impact on them. Trinity Oaks' Bucks for the Brave platform allows the loved ones and communities that support these heroes to share their stories and unite us all in honoring and recognizing their service to our great nation. We are proud to continue to be a part of this incredible program, getting these heroes in the outdoors for the hunt of a lifetime.

programme.title: Deer and Deer Hunting
programme.desc: Can the everyday landowner really manage their property well enough to hold and retain mature bucks year after year? From habitat improvements and food plots to pressure control and property layout, discover what truly influences a buck’s decision to stay—or stray.

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: Overview: Across the country, deer season to many Americans is a rite of passage. For generations, Winchester has been the trusted ammunition choice for hunters…and it’s been a decade since the game-changing Deer Season XP whitetail line was introduced. In this episode, we’ll celebrate the many successes by Team Winchester hunters in the field during whitetail and mule deer firearm seasons across the United States. GUESTS: Melissa Bachman (Team Winchester) Mark Drury (Team Winchester) Nikki Boxler (Team Winchester) Chris Keefer (Team Winchester) Casey Keefer (Team Winchester) Jason Gilbertson (Team Winchester)

programme.title: Hard Truths of Conservation
programme.desc: Human encroachment and pressure from poaching are the greatest threats to wildlife in Africa. Dan Cabela has traveled to Tanzania to see how Robin Hurt safaris manages two large conservation areas in the face of these daily challenges.

programme.title: We Caught That

programme.title: Local Knowledge
programme.desc: After a few incredible days targeting redfish on the inshore waters of Venice, Louisiana, Eric and Moe Newman bring Rush to the nearshore oil rigs — and that’s where things get wild. The rigs are holding GIANT mangrove snapper, bruiser cobia, and hard-fighting jacks… but getting them to the boat is a whole different story. Fishing heavy structure means short strikes, busted hooks, shredded leaders, and fish that know exactly where to run. Around these rigs it’s not just fishing — it’s full-blown combat. If you want to land one of these beasts, you better bring extra hooks, heavy tackle, and a whole lot of patience. Easier said than done. Venice, Louisiana continues to prove why it’s one of the most action-packed fisheries in the country.

programme.title: Local Knowledge
programme.desc: Season 10 is a wrap! Rush takes a moment to look back on some of his favorite moments from battling giant snook and feisty tarpon in Florida, to overnighting at Catalina Island, and chasing bull redfish in Louisiana. This season was all about getting out there with good friends, making new ones, and enjoying the sport we love. An unforgettable year filled with adventure, laughs, and epic days on the water.

Precision, power, and teamwork are put to the test as the road to the Crown begins.

programme.title: TRP
programme.desc: In this episode of the Tom Roland Podcast, brought to you by Danco, Captain Harley Hunt provides essential boating advice on how to determine if you're dragging anchor. Harley explains the importance of marking your GPS when setting the anchor and monitoring the distance to ensure you are stationary. He emphasizes the significance of re-anchoring if dragging occurs and shares the Coast Guard's recommended scope ratio for anchoring. Additionally, Harley discusses the benefits of using more chain to help the anchor dig in better. Tune in for valuable tips whether you're an experienced or beginner boater.Watch now to enhance your skills and improve your fishing game! programme.desc: Mark Peterson sets his sights on a Free-Range New Zealand Red Stag, but it doesn't come easy. Dense fog, steep terrain, and thick vegetation make finding a mature stag no easy task. On this hunt, Mark was unable to get his firearms import permit in time to take his Gunwerks rifle, so he'd again have to borrow from his guide.

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: Join Benny Spies of Team Winchester as he shares stories of his upbringing in South Dakota. From cherished family photos to duck seasons of the past, learn how a passion for hunting was ignited and became a way of life for Benny and his family.

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: Over a decade after the game changing Longbeard XR was introduced by Winchester, the wait is finally over for their next ground breaking turkey load. First watch the jaw dropping Longbeard TSS pattern board testing, then see it go to work on some spring gobblers.

programme.title: Wildbound by Forloh
programme.desc: In this episode Andy goes back to his home farm in Alabama for deer and quail hunting. Not only does he make incredible dishes with his former ‘masterchef’ contestant Alex which he serves to local children in need but he explains how hunting is never about “just the hunt” but about the camaraderie, friendship and memories made through the pursuit of the harvest.

programme.title: Huntstand Presents
programme.desc: The West Texas desert has a way of pulling you in. In Desert Ghost, Will Cooper heads west to Marathon, Texas, with longtime friend and mentor Cody Roberts to hunt aoudad in one of the most remote landscapes in the state. Rooted in family legacy, this film explores Cody’s deep ties to the desert, the story behind Desert Ghost Distillery, and the mystique of a western oasis near Big Bend National Park. More than a hunt, Desert Ghost dives into the role of aoudad management and its importance to desert bighorn sheep conservation efforts led by Texas Parks and Wildlife and the Wild Sheep Foundation. Sometimes, to protect what belongs here — you have to chase the ghosts. You really do have to be tough, determined, or one lucky bastard to make it out in West Texas.

programme.title: Chasin' The Sun
programme.desc: Meredith McCord arrives in late spring and joins Capt. Justin Leake on the hunt for a fish she’s never caught before. Lo and behold, it’s a day fit for a king.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: It’s that time again—when the boys from StruttinBuck load up and head out of Arkansas, bound for Fairfield, Texas and the FE Hill Ranch. Another year, another hunt, and another chapter in a tradition that’s grown into something far bigger than the hunt itself. This annual father-son trip is built on early mornings, shared stories, and moments that stick with you long after the season ends. It’s the memories made over the years that keep calling them back—and this year will be no different.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: We are blessed for the opportunity to follow along the guys from Trinity Oaks for Bucks for the Brave. The men and women that served in our armed services and as first responders, had jobs that required the, to be willing to sacrifice their own lives every day, in order to protect or save someone else's. Not many of us can say that, nor understand what that truly means. The things they've had to see, and the sacrifices they've made in their daily lives, can have a profound impact on them. Trinity Oaks' Bucks for the Brave platform allows the loved ones and communities that support these heroes to share their stories and unite us all in honoring and recognizing their service to our great nation. We are proud to continue to be a part of this incredible program, getting these heroes in the outdoors for the hunt of a lifetime.

programme.title: Deer and Deer Hunting
programme.desc: Can the everyday landowner really manage their property well enough to hold and retain mature bucks year after year? From habitat improvements and food plots to pressure control and property layout, discover what truly influences a buck’s decision to stay—or stray.

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: Overview: Across the country, deer season to many Americans is a rite of passage. For generations, Winchester has been the trusted ammunition choice for hunters…and it’s been a decade since the game-changing Deer Season XP whitetail line was introduced. In this episode, we’ll celebrate the many successes by Team Winchester hunters in the field during whitetail and mule deer firearm seasons across the United States. GUESTS: Melissa Bachman (Team Winchester) Mark Drury (Team Winchester) Nikki Boxler (Team Winchester) Chris Keefer (Team Winchester) Casey Keefer (Team Winchester) Jason Gilbertson (Team Winchester)

programme.title: Hard Truths of Conservation
programme.desc: Human encroachment and pressure from poaching are the greatest threats to wildlife in Africa. Dan Cabela has traveled to Tanzania to see how Robin Hurt safaris manages two large conservation areas in the face of these daily challenges.

programme.title: We Caught That
programme.desc: In this episode, Capt. Jason Dozier teams up with (name) to chase the elusive Hogfish. With the Hogfish proving elusive, they manage to turn the day into a success, filling the box with an impressive haul and reeling in a few massive catches along the way.

programme.title: Local Knowledge
programme.desc: After a few incredible days targeting redfish on the inshore waters of Venice, Louisiana, Eric and Moe Newman bring Rush to the nearshore oil rigs — and that’s where things get wild. The rigs are holding GIANT mangrove snapper, bruiser cobia, and hard-fighting jacks… but getting them to the boat is a whole different story. Fishing heavy structure means short strikes, busted hooks, shredded leaders, and fish that know exactly where to run. Around these rigs it’s not just fishing — it’s full-blown combat. If you want to land one of these beasts, you better bring extra hooks, heavy tackle, and a whole lot of patience. Easier said than done. Venice, Louisiana continues to prove why it’s one of the most action-packed fisheries in the country.

programme.title: Local Knowledge
programme.desc: Season 10 is a wrap! Rush takes a moment to look back on some of his favorite moments from battling giant snook and feisty tarpon in Florida, to overnighting at Catalina Island, and chasing bull redfish in Louisiana. This season was all about getting out there with good friends, making new ones, and enjoying the sport we love. An unforgettable year filled with adventure, laughs, and epic days on the water.

programme.title: Super Retriever
programme.desc: The Super Retriever Series Crown Championship brings together the nation’s top canine athletes and handlers in Shreveport-Bossier City, Louisiana. In Part 1 of the Amateur Division, teams face off in challenging Retriever Trials alongside high-energy SRS Super Dock competition. Precision, power, and teamwork are put to the test as the road to the Crown begins.

programme.desc: A family devastated by grief meets a woman who helps heal the loss.

programme.title: Deal of the Century
programme.desc: An arms dealer attempts to sell war planes to a South American dictator.

programme.title: Mars Attacks!
programme.desc: Martians delight in incinerating humans in Tim Burton's sci-fi send-up.

programme.title: Club Paradise
programme.desc: A Chicago fireman holds onto his dream of owning a tropical island resort.

programme.title: Swept From the Sea
programme.desc: Amy, a servant girl, and Yanko, the sole survivor of a capsized ship bound for America, begin a fiery affair at the end of the nineteenth century.

programme.title: The Princess & the Marine
programme.desc: Based on a true story about the forbidden love between a Mormon lance corporal and a Bahraini royal family member bound for an arranged marriage.

programme.title: No Small Affair
programme.desc: When Charles Cummings sees a down-on-her-luck rock singer, Laura Victor, through his camera lens, he immediately falls head over heels in love.

programme.title: Crossroads
programme.desc: A young guitar prodigy seeks a reclusive blues master to learn a lost song, setting him on a journey into the heart of the blues tradition.

programme.title: Peggy Sue Got Married
programme.desc: An unhappy woman named Peggy Sue tries to change her future when she inadvertently travels back in time to her senior year of high school.

programme.title: Aloha
programme.desc: A military contractor on assignment in Hawaii reconnects with a long-ago love while unexpectedly falling for his hard-charging Air Force watchdog.

programme.title: The Karate Kid
programme.desc: A troubled teenager finds guidance in an unorthodox karate master, who teaches him wisdom and self-control, helping him conquer his deepest fears.

programme.title: The Karate Kid Part II
programme.desc: After learning that his father is dying, karate master Mr. Miyagi (Noriyuki "Pat" Morita) returns home to Okinawa, bringing his protege, Daniel (Ralph Macchio), with him. In Japan, Miyagi is surprised to discover that his old sweetheart, Yukie (Nobu McCarthy), has remained single. Meanwhile, Daniel is attracted to Yukie's niece, Kumiko (Tamlyn Tomita). But romance must be put on hold while Daniel and Miyagi deal with local bullies and long-harbored grudges.

programme.title: The Karate Kid Part III
programme.desc: Cobra Kai karate instructor John Kreese (Martin Kove) is still brooding over the defeat handed to him by Daniel LaRusso (Ralph Macchio) and Mr. Miyagi (Noriyuki "Pat" Morita). With his slimy partner, Terry Silver (Thomas Ian Griffith), Kreese plans an intricate payback that involves hiring a mean-spirited ringer (Sean Kanan) to rob Daniel of his championship title. When Mr. Miyagi refuses to train Daniel, even under threat, the boy makes the mistake of turning to Terry for guidance.

programme.title: Legends of the Fall
programme.desc: In early 20th-century Montana, a family is torn apart as two brothers fall for the same woman, igniting a rivalry that strains their bond.

programme.title: A River Runs Through It
programme.desc: The hit film version of the bestselling book about a Montana family and the relationship between two brothers with completely different personalities.

programme.title: Gattaca
programme.desc: In a genetically engineered future, a naturally born man assumes the identity of an elite citizen to pursue his goal of space travel.

programme.title: Cold Comes The Night
programme.desc: A struggling motel owner and her daughter are taken hostage by a nearly blind career criminal to be his eyes as he attempts to retrieve his cash package from a crooked cop.

programme.title: The Principal
programme.desc: After taking a bat to the Porsche of his ex-wife’s divorce attorney, teacher Rick Latimer is punished with a principal post at a violent high school.

programme.title: Chances Are
programme.desc: A man's love for his pregnant wife, Corinne Jeffries (Cybill Shepherd), is interrupted when a car accident sends him to heaven. He is reincarnated, however, and two decades later he is a writer named Alex Finch (Robert Downey Jr.). But when Alex starts dating Miranda Jeffries (Mary Stuart Masterson) -- his all-grown-up daughter from his previous life -- he remembers his love for Corinne. This spells trouble for his past-life best friend, Philip Train (Ryan O'Neal), who is now pursuing Corinne.

programme.title: Spanglish
programme.desc: A celebrity chef balancing a high-profile career and a family gets a lesson in life and culture clashes from his new housekeeper.

programme.title: Country Strong
programme.desc: A fallen country star tries to revive her career with help from her songwriter husband and a rising artist whose fan base is quickly growing.

programme.title: Premium Rush
programme.desc: After a NYC bike messenger collects an envelope, he’s hunted down by a corrupt cop who’s desperate to get his hands on the package, at any cost.

programme.title: Lake Placid
programme.desc: At a serene Maine lake, a 30-foot reptilian eating machine hungers for a fragile paleontologist on her first field assignment and a tasty billionaire.

programme.title: Godzilla
programme.desc: When a giant monster created by nuclear tests descends upon New York, it's up to the U.S. Army and an odd team of investigators to stop the creature.

programme.title: Fail-Safe
programme.desc: When a system glitch accidentally sends nuclear-armed U.S. bombers to Moscow, the President races against time to prevent a global apocalypse.

programme.title: The Jolson Story
programme.desc: At the turn of the 20th century, young Asa Yoelson (Scotty Beckett) decides to go against the wishes of his cantor father (Ludwig Donath) and pursue a career in show business. Gradually working his way up through the vaudeville ranks, Asa -- now calling himself Al Jolson (Larry Parks) -- joins a blackface minstrel troupe and soon builds a reputation as a consummate performer. But as his career grows in size, so does his ego, resulting in battles in business as well as in his personal life.

programme.title: Boots Malone
programme.desc: A down-on-his-luck jockey's agent takes a wealthy, yet neglected kid under his wing, and, with the help of others, turns him into a champion.

programme.title: Murderer's Row
programme.desc: Secret agent Matt Helm must rescue the kidnapped inventor of an ultra-destructive "helio beam" capable of destroying Washington, D.C.

programme.title: Harriet Craig
programme.desc: A cold, perfectionist wife will stop at nothing to have her house and life run as she wishes, even if it means controlling her husband's every move.

programme.title: How to Save a Marriage and Ruin Your Life
programme.desc: A dedicated bachelor takes it upon himself to save his best friend’s marriage by seducing his mistress, only to wind up in his own romantic conundrum.

programme.title: Dr. Strangelove Or: How I Learned to Stop Worrying and Love the Bomb
programme.desc: Based on Peter George's book "Red Alert."

programme.title: Mr. Smith Goes to Washington
programme.desc: A youth leader and idealist fights false accusations and corruption from the political machine that engineered his appointment to the U.S. Senate.

programme.title: A Man for All Seasons
programme.desc: From the Robert Bolt play.

programme.title: 711 Ocean Drive
programme.desc: A phone company repairman bites off more than he can chew when he agrees to create a system that broadcasts race track results for a crime boss.

programme.title: Love Has Many Faces
programme.desc: Wealthy Kit Jordan (Lana Turner) and her dissolute husband, Pete (Cliff Robertson), are in Acapulco, Mexico, when a corpse washes onto the beach. The deceased was a lover of Kit's and a friend of Pete's, so the police question the Jordans. Then the dead man's girlfriend (Stefanie Powers) arrives, and she and Pete have an affair. Luckily, Kit has her choice of men to distract her. There is gigolo Hank (Hugh O'Brian), bullfighter Julian (Carlos Montalban) and, when in a pinch, even her husband.

programme.title: First Comes Courage
programme.desc: Beautiful Norwegian woman Nicole Larsen (Merle Oberon) is dedicated to the local resistance movement, and seduces Nazi officer Paul Dichter (Carl Esmond) in order to gain information that will help to defeat the Germans. Complicating matters for Nicole are both the difficulty of her loathsome undercover work and the appearance of an old flame, handsome English soldier Allan Lowell (Brian Aherne), who would like nothing more than to reunite with her.

programme.title: Passport to Suez
programme.desc: Master sleuth Lone Wolf (Warren William) outwits Nazi spies for canal plans in Egypt.

programme.title: Hot Blood
programme.desc: A gypsy woman who lives by decamping with marriage settlements from wealthy men meets her match in the handsome younger brother of a gypsy king.

programme.title: The Doctor Takes a Wife
programme.desc: A writer (Loretta Young) and a neurologist (Ray Milland) accept rumors of their marriage when each stands to gain from the mistake.

programme.title: Under the Yum-Yum Tree
programme.desc: The machinations of a lusty landlord threaten the bliss of a couple trying to determine their character compatibility by living together platonically.

programme.title: Holiday
programme.desc: Johnny (Cary Grant) seems like a solid match for Julia (Doris Nolan), the socialite daughter of Edward Seton (Henry Kolker). Assured that Johnny is a worthy suitor, Edward approves of the pairing. But, as Johnny's wanderlust surfaces -- he is more interested in traveling than in business -- Edward starts to have doubts. Johnny also begins to wonder if he might not be a better match for Linda (Katharine Hepburn), Julia's outspoken younger sister, with whom he has much more in common.

programme.title: Don't Raise the Bridge, Lower the River
programme.desc: A daydreamer enlists the help of his British con artist friend to steal and sell the plans for a valuable new oil drill in this spy thriller spoof.

programme.title: Oliver!
programme.desc: In 1830s London, orphan Oliver Twist escapes to join a band of young pickpockets led by the sly Fagin in this musical take on Dickens' classic tale.

programme.title: Guess Who's Coming to Dinner
programme.desc: A daughter visits her family to introduce them to her Black fiancé, whose kindness and loving demeanor changes the hearts of her reluctant parents.

programme.title: Three Hours to Kill
programme.desc: The inhabitants of a small western community turn on Jim Guthrie (Dana Andrews) when he becomes the prime suspect for a murder he didn't commit. Before getting a chance to prove his innocence, Jim is driven from his home and into the wilderness, where he is left for dead. Despite the danger, he comes back years later, discovering that he's still a wanted man. But a kindhearted local named Chris (Dianne Foster) believes his story, and together they begin a desperate search for the true culprit.

programme.title: Utah Blaine
programme.desc: A gunfighter helps a ranch owner in his attempt to prevent a renegade crew from taking over the territory.

programme.title: Traveling Saleswoman
programme.desc: A soapmaker's daughter (Joan Davis) goes to the Wild West, followed by her boyfriend (Andy Devine).

programme.title: Wyoming Renegades
programme.desc: An ex-convict (Phil Carey) and his girlfriend (Martha Hyer) cannot start over until they stop Butch Cassidy's (Gene Evans) gang.

programme.title: The Violent Men
programme.desc: Former Union soldier John Parrish (Glenn Ford) wants to sell his ranch and start a new life by settling down out east. However, the ruthless tactics of Lew Wilkison (Edward G. Robinson), the scheming businessman interested in his property, make Parrish reconsider the move, especially after one of his ranch hands is murdered. Adding to the increasingly tense conflict is Wilkison's wife, Martha (Barbara Stanwyck), who is cheating on her husband with his brother, Cole (Brian Keith).

programme.title: Return of the Durango Kid
programme.desc: A stagecoach passenger poses as a masked avenger to track down the thieves who robbed him and his companions.

programme.title: Stranger From Arizona
programme.desc: A quiet cowboy (Buck Jones) on a white horse saves a gal (Dorothy Fay) from cattle rustlers.

programme.title: The Taming of the West
programme.desc: A new lawman (Bill Elliott) punches out cattle rustlers who like to shoot sheriffs.

programme.title: The Tall T
programme.desc: The victims must outsmart their cruel captors after an outlaw, and his gang kidnap an heiress, her nervous husband, and a rancher.

programme.title: Daring Danger
programme.desc: A wounded cowboy (Tim McCoy) catches rustlers who use a trick branding iron.

programme.title: The Desperadoes
programme.desc: Sheriff Steve Upton (Randolph Scott) is a devoted lawman who works tirelessly to keep order in his small town, nestled within the Utah territory. So, when Cheyenne Rogers (Glenn Ford), an old friend with a criminal past, rolls into town, Upton is immediately on edge. Cheyenne assures his pal that all that's behind him now. But when the town bank is robbed, Cheyenne becomes the locals' number one suspect. He'll have to prove his innocence to save himself and his friend's reputation.

programme.title: King of the Wild Horses
programme.desc: A mustang stallion runs swiftly to the rescue when the boy who befriended him is endangered.

programme.title: Good Day for a Hanging
programme.desc: When the local sheriff is killed after a bank robbery, ex-lawman Ben Cutler (Fred MacMurray) collars the apparent killer, Eddie Campbell (Robert Vaughn). Because of his background as a local youth who spent time living with the Cutlers, Eddie gets the benefit of the doubt from Ben's daughter and his wife-to-be, Ruth (Margaret Hayes). Ben is firm in his belief that Eddie is guilty, and as the matter is debated in a divisive court battle, it will soon become clear whether or not he's right.

programme.title: Cowboy
programme.desc: A Chicago native yearning for the Old West discovers that the cowboy life is not as he'd imagined when he participates in a 3,000 mile cattle drive.

programme.title: The Desert Horseman
programme.desc: The Durango Kid (Charles Starrett) and his sidekick (Smiley Burnette) catch up to payroll robbers.

programme.title: Gunfighters
programme.desc: Brazos Kane, a gunslinger who's vowed to kill no more, goes to work for a land baron on a ranch, where he is unjustly accused of murder.

programme.title: Ambush at Tomahawk Gap
programme.desc: Flaming arrows stop four ex-convicts searching a ghost town for their buried loot.

programme.title: Last of the Comanches
programme.desc: A cavalry sergeant (Broderick Crawford) leads soldiers, civilians and an Indian (Johnny Stewart) on a desert trek.

programme.title: King of the Wild Horses
programme.desc: A mustang stallion runs swiftly to the rescue when the boy who befriended him is endangered.

programme.title: Battle of Rogue River
programme.desc: A cavalry major (George Montgomery) makes peace with Indians despite an agitator (Richard Denning) working for carpetbaggers.

programme.title: Avenging Waters
programme.desc: A lone cowboy (Ken Maynard) gets the bad guys and wins the gal (Beth Marion).

programme.title: Trail of the Rustlers
programme.desc: When a member of the villainous Mahoney family is slain while escaping the jail cell he'd been put in by Steve Armitage (Charles Starrett), aka the Durango Kid, the dead man's relatives vow to take down the Kid by any means necessary. Chick Mahoney (Don Harvey) poses as the Kid and tries to ruin the cowboy's reputation by stealing from nearby ranchers, but with the help of sidekick Smiley Burnette (Smiley Burnette), the Kid sets out to retrieve the stolen goods and repair his good name.

programme.title: The Mountain Men
programme.desc: Two old-time Wyoming fur trappers (Charlton Heston, Brian Keith) try to protect a runaway Indian (Victoria Racimo) from her angry ex-mate.

programme.title: The Longest Drive
programme.desc: After a decade apart, a pair of brothers reunites to find their long-lost sister, who was kidnapped as a child and is now living with a native tribe.

programme.title: Gunman's Walk
programme.desc: Rancher Lee Hackett (Van Heflin) spends so much time trying to keep the violent streak of his son Ed (Tab Hunter) in check, he neglects the needs of his other son Davy (James Darren). When Ed's temper results in the death of an innocent Native American, Lee tries his best to cover his son's tracks by making a deal with unscrupulous horse trader Jensen (Ray Teal), who falsely testifies on Ed's behalf. But it becomes clear that Ed can't change, and a shootout between father and son ensues.

programme.title: Headin' East
programme.desc: A cowboy discards his chaps and saddle for a suit and tie when he travels to the big city to investigate a produce racket that has been ruining his father's lettuce shipments.

programme.title: Gun Fury
programme.desc: California-bound rancher Ben Warren (Rock Hudson) is shot and left for dead when a demented ex-Confederate, Frank Slayton (Phil Carey), kidnaps his Southern bride-to-be (Donna Reed). Revived, Ben sets out to rescue her. Along the way, he picks up Jess Burgess (Leo Gordon), a slighted former member of Slayton's gang, and American Indian local Johash (Pat Hogan), who has his own beef with the outlaws. Together they embark on a perilous chase toward the Mexican border to stop Slayton.

programme.title: Fury at Gunsight Pass
programme.desc: One bank robber (David Brian) double-crosses another (Neville Brand), giving the undertaker and others a chance at the loot.

programme.desc: Judy Garland's 1944 musical dramatizes turn-of-the-century family life.

programme.title: Easy to Love
programme.desc: Williams wins her boss over when she starts an affair with another man.

programme.title: Around the World In 80 Days
programme.desc: An adventurous Englishman wagers that he can circle the globe in 80 days.

programme.title: Strangers on a Train
programme.desc: Two strangers strike a deadly pact in this Hitchcock classic.

programme.title: The Man Who Came to Dinner
programme.desc: After an accident, a literary critic recovers at the home of the family he was visiting, but his unplanned stay turns their lives upside down.

programme.title: The Charge of the Light Brigade
programme.desc: Two brothers love the same woman at a perilous Indian outpost.

programme.title: Abbott and Costello Meet Captain Kidd

programme.title: Period of Adjustment
programme.desc: A newlywed couple's honeymoon is disrupted by a friends' marital problems.

programme.title: Onionhead
programme.desc: A lazy student enlists in the Navy expecting to sit out World War II.

programme.title: Ben-Hur
programme.desc: A rebellious Israelite prince crosses paths with Jesus Christ.

programme.title: Bad Day at Black Rock
programme.desc: A man seeks for a Japanese American farmer to whose son he owes his life.

programme.title: The Music Man

programme.desc: Rebel soldiers try to hijack a Union gold shipment.

programme.title: Battle of the Bulge
programme.desc: A crack Nazi unit holds off the Allies during World War II.

programme.title: MacGyver
programme.desc: Mac and the team attempt to extinguish a massive oil wellhead fire set by a rebel group in Nigeria.

programme.title: MacGyver
programme.desc: MacGyver uses a heater and hot sauce to help a key presidential candidate in Ecuador receive a heart transplant he desperately needs after a rebel group tries to sabotage delivery of the organ.

programme.title: MacGyver
programme.desc: MacGyver and Jack "steal" a priceless painting to draw out an infamous stolen art dealer; Riley's estranged father asks to be part of her life; Matty sends Bozer to a spy training camp.

programme.title: MacGyver
programme.desc: Mac and the team follow mercenaries who are transporting stolen lethal nerve gas; Jack confronts Elwood about Riley; Bozer is pushed to his limit in spy school.

programme.title: MacGyver
programme.desc: Mac must help college students stranded on a research ship in the Arctic Ocean; Jack kidnaps Elwood to find out what he's up to; Bozer's feelings toward Leanna grow stronger.

programme.title: MacGyver
programme.desc: The LAPD interrupts the team's Christmas celebration to arrest Mac for terrorism when they find a body in a building destroyed by a bomb Mac built.

programme.title: MacGyver
programme.desc: When Mac's nemesis rigs Mac's house with explosives and traps him and Jack inside, Mac uses a karaoke mic and an audio transformer to let the team know that the bomb is a distraction for a bigger one.

programme.title: Bones
programme.desc: While in the hospital after being shot at the lab, Brennan struggles with visions of her mother that defy her usual logic; evidence may link Brennan's attacker to the Jeffersonian.

programme.title: Bones
programme.desc: As the team investigates the death of a seemingly innocent 15-year-old boy, they learn that he was involved in illegal activity.

programme.title: Bones
programme.desc: When a well-educated but eccentric intern starts working at the Jeffersonian, Brennan tries to be more open-minded about scientific questions.

programme.title: Bones
programme.desc: The team investigates the death of a young man whose remains were found at a hazardous waste disposal facility; Cam and Arastoo struggle to keep their relationship secret; Brennan and Booth try to decide where to take their vacation.

programme.title: Bones
programme.desc: The investigation into the death of a former Marine leads the team to a group obsessed with preparing for the apocalypse; Sweets finds an apartment.

programme.title: Bones
programme.desc: The team investigates when an undercover cop turns up dead with a bag of diamonds inside his body; Brennan struggles to be likable as the subject of a documentary film.
